properties.description: The Art & Humanities Building is home to the Kopleff Recital Hall as well as to the classrooms, computer design labs and studios of the Ernest G. Welch School of Art and Design.
properties.name: Arts & Humanities

properties.description: This building houses Information Systems & Technology , Facilities Management Services, RCB's Center for Ethics and Corporate Responsibility, The Commerce Club and the Atlanta Press Club.
properties.name: Bennett A. Brown Commerce Building

properties.description: The building was constructed in 1966 and houses numerous classrooms for various departments.
properties.name: Classroom South

properties.description: The College of Education at Georgia State University is known for its outstanding programs in education, counseling and sports-related fields. The college prepares approximately 500 teachers annually
properties.name: College of Education

properties.description: This building houses the ROTC units and several maintenance departments.
properties.name: Courtland Building

properties.description: Dahlberg Hall (previously Alumni Hall) is home to university Administration and the Alumni Association. It also includes the Welcome Center, the official reception area for visitors to the University.
properties.name: Dahlberg Hall

properties.description: G deck entrances are located at Courtland St., Collins Street, and Central Ave. It is available only to GSU faculty, staff and visitors.
properties.name: G Deck

properties.description: Nine of GSU's 24 Greek organizations live in the new development, which consists of nine three-story townhomes of varying capacity, housing a total of 139 students.
properties.name: Greek Housing

properties.description: Haas-Howell houses the School of Music. Constructed in 1920 and occupied by the university since 1994, it contains School of Music administrative and faculty offices, recording studios, a state-of-the-art media and technology center and classrooms.
properties.name: Haas-Howell Building

properties.description: This facility houses many general purpose classrooms and lecture halls with some office space. Classrooms are spacious and equipped with current the latest in audio-visual and data technology. Shops and eateries are located outside.
properties.name: Helen M. Aderhold Learning Center

properties.description: The largest business school in the South and part of a major research institution, the J. Mack Robinson College of Business at Georgia State University is located in Atlanta, an epicenter of business.
properties.name: J. Mack Robinson College of Business
properties.wikipedia: en:J. Mack Robinson College of Business Administration Building

properties.description: K-Deck is located at Gilmer Street and a Budget Card or cash with a valid student parking permit may be used for access. K deck is available for student use.
properties.name: K Deck

properties.description: Langdale Hall houses the departments of Political Science, English, Sociology, and Modern and Classical Languages, the offices of the College of Arts and Sciences, as well as many general use classrooms.
properties.name: Langdale Hall

properties.description: The Georgia State University Library provides leadership in accessing and using information for Georgia's urban research university.
properties.name: Library North

properties.description: Library South houses an large assortment of the library's collections.
properties.name: Library South

properties.description: No parking attendant on duty. This deck is for residents of the Lofts, Greek Housing, Freshmen Hall, and Non-Resident Semester permits, some faculty and staff.
properties.name: Loft Parking

properties.description: The M deck is located at Auditorium Place and is restricted to students parking with a semester permit, reserved staff/faculty, day care drop off and visitors to the university
properties.name: M Deck

properties.description: N Deck has no reserved handicap accessible spots. There is no elevator to maneuver between floors.
properties.name: N Deck

properties.description: This building holds classrooms and teaching labs for several science departments. In addition, it houses the main offices of the Departments of Chemistry and Physics and Astronomy, and a shop facility that makes custom-designed equipment for researchers.
properties.name: Natural Science Center

properties.description: The center is a 347,000 square-foot space that houses programs in biology, chemistry, nutrition, nursing, physical therapy,Â public healthÂ and respiratory therapy. It is also home to the Neuroscience Institute, Center for Inflammation, Immunity and Infe
properties.name: Petit Science Center

properties.description: Anchored in the historic Fairlie-Poplar district, The Rialto Center at GSU provides a venue for visitors to enjoy performances ranging from theatre to dance to spoken word to film and to music, including jazz, blues, classical and world arts.
properties.name: Rialto Center for the Arts

properties.description: S Deck has no reserved handicap accessible spots. There is no elevator to maneuver between floors.
properties.name: S Deck

properties.description: The spaces in this building are assigned to Chemistry, Biology, Physics, and Astronomy.
properties.name: Science Annex

properties.description: Athletic teams at Georgia State include football, baseball, basketball, cross-country, golf, soccer, tennis, and track & field for men and basketball, cross-country, golf, soccer, softball, tennis, track & field, and volleyball for women.
properties.name: Sports Annex

properties.description: The Sports Arena serves as headquarters for Georgia State Athletics, housing offices for head coaches in all sports as well as the athletic director's office and various administrative departments.
properties.name: Sports Arena

properties.description: Constructed in 1923 and occupied by the university in 1984, this building is home to School of Music classrooms, faculty offices, rehearsal rooms for small instrumental ensembles, student and faculty lounges, and recording studios.
properties.name: Standard Building

properties.description: The state-of-the-art recreation center contains a climbing wall, racquetball and squash courts, a basketball court, a game room with billiard tables, table tennis, extensive work-out areas for cardio and weight training, a pool, and many other services.
properties.name: Student Recreation Center

properties.description: T Deck entrance is located at 43 Auburn Avenue.
properties.name: T Deck

properties.description: Ten Park Place houses a variety of programs from the College of Arts and Sciences. The Office of International Affairs, Georgia State's central international education office with information on the study abroad programs for students, is here.
properties.name: Ten Park Place

properties.description: The bookstore is the official headquarters for textbooks, class supplies, Georgia State University insignia merchandise, gift items, general books, computers, software, and snacks.
properties.name: University Bookstore

properties.description: The University Center was built in 1963 and is home to Cinefest, fraternity and sorority offices, and food services.
properties.name: Student Center West

properties.description: The Lofts houses approximately 450 residents. Students can enjoy fully furnished loft-style apartments that blend urban style with modern-day conveniences.
properties.name: University Lofts

properties.description: The Urban Life Building houses Georgia State University's College of Law, College of Law Library, College of Human and Health Sciences and the College of Arts and Sciences Psychology Department.
properties.name: Urban Life Building

properties.description: 34 Peachtree is home to the departments of Applied Linguistics (12th floor), Computer Science (14th floor), Philosophy (11th floor),Religious Studies (11th Floor), History (20th Floor), and Middle East Institute (20th Floor), Passport Health (19th floor)
properties.name: 34 Peachtree
